volumeTitle: Antigone. The Women of Trachis. Philoctetes. Oedipus at Colonus
label: Oedipus at Colonus (Section 333a - Section 468)

sections.label: Section 333a
sections.original: καὶ λόγων γʼ αὐτάγγελος ,
sections.translation: Yes , and to bring you news by my own mouth , with the only faithful servant that I had .

sections.label: Section 335
sections.original: οἱ δʼ αὐθόμαιμοι ποῦ νεανίαι πονεῖν ;
sections.translation: And where are the young men , your brothers , in our need ?

sections.label: Section 336
sections.original: εἴσʼ οὗπέρ εἰσι · δεινὰ τἀν κείνοις τανῦν .
sections.translation: They are where they are ; their circumstances now are terrible .

sections.label: Section 337
sections.original: ὦ πάντʼ ἐκείνω τοῖς ἐν Αἰγύπτῳ νόμοις
sections.translation: True image of the ways of Egypt that they show in their spirit and their life ! For there the men sit weaving in the house ,

sections.label: Section 340
sections.original: θακοῦσιν ἱστουργοῦντες , αἱ δὲ σύννομοι
sections.translation: but the wives go forth to win the daily bread . And in your case , my daughters , those to whom these toils belonged keep the house at home like maidens , while you two , in their place , bear your poor father ’ s woes .

sections.label: Section 345
sections.original: ὑπερπονεῖτον . ἡ μὲν ἐξ ὅτου νέας
sections.translation: The one , from the time when her youth was past and she came into her strength , has always been this old man ’ s guide in weary wanderings , often roaming , hungry and barefoot , through the wild woods , often battered by rains and scorching sun .

sections.label: Section 350
sections.original: πολλοῖσι δʼ ὄμβροις ἡλίου τε καύμασιν
sections.translation:
And the comforts of home , poor girl , she holds in the second place , so long as her father should have her care .
And you , my child , in former days came forth , bringing your father , unknown to the Cadmeans , all the oracles that had been given concerning Oedipus .

sections.label: Section 355
sections.original: ἃ τοῦδʼ ἐχρήσθη σώματος , φύλαξ τέ μου
sections.translation: You became a faithful guardian on my behalf , when I was being driven from the land . Now , in turn , what new tidings have you brought your father , Ismene ? On what mission have you set forth from home ? For you do not come empty-handed , I know well ,

sections.label: Section 360
sections.original: ἔξοιδα , μὴ οὐχὶ δεῖμʼ ἐμοὶ φέρουσά τι .
sections.translation: or without some cause of fear for me .

sections.label: Section 361
sections.original: ἐγὼ τὰ μὲν παθήμαθʼ ἅπαθον , πάτερ ,
sections.translation: The sufferings that I bore , father , in seeking where you dwelt , I will pass by ; I would not renew the pain in the recital .

sections.label: Section 365
sections.original: ἃ δʼ ἀμφὶ τοῖν σοῖν δυσμόροιν παίδοιν κακὰ
sections.translation: But the evils that now beset your ill-fated sons — it is of these that I have come to tell . At first it was their decision that the throne should be left to Creon , and the city spared pollution , when they thought calmly about the ancient blight on our race ,

sections.label: Section 370
sections.original: οἵα κατέσχε τὸν σὸν ἄθλιον δόμον ·
sections.translation: and how it has clung to your unfortunate house . But now , moved by some god and by sinful heart , an evil strife has seized them — thrice-deluded !— to grasp at rule and the power of a tyrant . And the younger son has stripped the elder , Polyneices , of the throne ,

sections.label: Section 375
sections.original: τὸν πρόσθε γεννηθέντα Πολυνείκη θρόνων
sections.translation: and has driven him from his fatherland . But he , as the widespread rumor says among us , has gone to the valley of Argos as an exile , and is taking to himself a new marriage connection , and warriors for his friends , intending that Argos soon get hold of the Cadmean land ,

sections.label: Section 380
sections.original: ὡς αὐτίκʼ Ἄργος ἢ τὸ Καδμείων πέδον
sections.translation: or send its praises to the sky . These are not empty words , my father , but terrible deeds ; and where the gods will have pity on your grief , I cannot tell .

sections.label: Section 385
sections.original: ἤδη γὰρ ἔσχες ἐλπίδʼ ὡς ἐμοῦ θεοὺς
sections.translation: What , had you come to hope that the gods would ever have concern enough for me to give me rescue ?

sections.label: Section 387
sections.original: ἔγωγε τοῖς νὺν γʼ , ὦ πάτερ , μαντεύμασιν .
sections.translation: Yes , that is my hope , father , from the present oracles .

sections.label: Section 388
sections.original: ποίοισι τούτοις ; τί δὲ τεθέσπισται , τέκνον ;
sections.translation: What are they ? What has been prophesied , my child ?

sections.label: Section 389
sections.original: σὲ τοῖς ἐκεῖ ζητητὸν ἀνθρώποις ποτὲ
sections.translation: That you will be desired some day , in life and death , by the men of that land ,

sections.label: Section 390
sections.original: θανόντʼ ἔσεσθαι ζῶντά τʼ εὐσοίας χάριν .
sections.translation: for their safety ’ s sake .

sections.label: Section 391
sections.original: τίς δʼ ἂν τοιοῦδʼ ὑπʼ ἀνδρὸς εὖ πράξειεν ἄν ;
sections.translation: And who could profit from such a one as I ?

sections.label: Section 392
sections.original: ἐν σοὶ τὰ κείνων φασὶ γίγνεσθαι κράτη .
sections.translation: Their power , it is said , proves to be in your hands .

sections.label: Section 393
sections.original: ὅτʼ οὐκέτʼ εἰμί , τηνικαῦτʼ ἄρʼ εἴμʼ ἀνήρ ;
sections.translation: When I no longer exist , then I am a man ?

sections.label: Section 394
sections.original: νῦν γὰρ θεοί σʼ ὀρθοῦσι , πρόσθε δʼ ὤλλυσαν .
sections.translation: Yes , for the gods now raise you up ; but before they worked your ruin .

sections.label: Section 395
sections.original: γέροντα δʼ ὀρθοῦν φλαῦρον ὃς νέος πέσῃ .
sections.translation: It is a paltry thing to raise up age , when youth was ruined .

sections.label: Section 396
sections.original: καὶ μὴν Κρέοντά γʼ ἴσθι σοι τούτων χάριν
sections.translation: Well , know at least that Creon will come to you on this account — and soon , not late .

sections.label: Section 398
sections.original: ὅπως τί δράσῃ , θύγατερ ; ἑρμήνευέ μοι .
sections.translation: With what purpose , daughter ? Interpret that to me .

sections.label: Section 399
sections.original: ὥς σʼ ἄγχι γῆς στήσωσι Καδμείας , ὅπως
sections.translation: To plant you near the Cadmean land , so that they may have you in their power ,

sections.label: Section 400
sections.original: κρατῶσι μὲν σοῦ , γῆς δὲ μὴ ʼμβαίνῃς ὅρων .
sections.translation: while you may not set foot within their borders .

sections.label: Section 401
sections.original: ἡ δʼ ὠφέλησις τίς θύρασι κειμένου ;
sections.translation: And how can I profit them while I rest beyond their gates ?

sections.label: Section 402
sections.original: κείνοις ὁ τύμβος δυστυχῶν ὁ σὸς βαρύς .
sections.translation: Your tomb contains a curse for them , if it should suffer misfortune .

sections.label: Section 403
sections.original: κἄνευ θεοῦ τις τοῦτό γʼ ἂν γνώμῃ μάθοι .
sections.translation: I need no god to help my wits so far .

sections.label: Section 404
sections.original: τούτου χάριν τοίνυν σε προσθέσθαι πέλας
sections.translation: For this reason , therefore , they wish to get you as their neighbor ;

sections.label: Section 405
sections.original: χώρας θέλουσι , μηδʼ ἵνʼ ἂν σαυτοῦ κρατοῖς .
sections.translation: but in a place where they will have you at their mercy .

sections.label: Section 406
sections.original: ἦ καὶ κατασκιῶσι Θηβαίᾳ κόνει ;
sections.translation: Will they really cover me in Theban dust ?

sections.label: Section 407
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐκ ἐᾷ τοὔμφυλον αἷμά σʼ , ὦ πάτερ .
sections.translation: No , the guilt of related blood forbids you , father .

sections.label: Section 408
sections.original: οὐκ ἆρʼ ἐμοῦ γε μὴ κρατήσωσίν ποτε .
sections.translation: Then never will they become my masters .

sections.label: Section 409
sections.original: ἔσται ποτʼ ἆρα τοῦτο Καδμείοις βάρος .
sections.translation: Someday then this will be a grief for the Cadmeans .

sections.label: Section 410
sections.original: ποίας φανείσης , ὦ τέκνον , συναλλαγῆς ;
sections.translation: In what conjunction of events , my child ?

sections.label: Section 411
sections.original: τῆς σῆς ὑπʼ ὀργῆς , σοῖς ὅταν στῶσιν τάφοις .
sections.translation: Under the power of your anger , when they stand at your tomb .

sections.label: Section 412
sections.original: ἃ δʼ ἐννέπεις , κλύουσα τοῦ λέγεις , τέκνον ;
sections.translation: And who has told you this , my child ?

sections.label: Section 413
sections.original: ἀνδρῶν θεωρῶν Δελφικῆς ἀφʼ ἑστίας .
sections.translation: Sacred envoys , from the Delphian hearth .

sections.label: Section 414
sections.original: καὶ ταῦτʼ ἐφʼ ἡμῖν Φοῖβος εἰρηκὼς κυρεῖ ;
sections.translation: And has Phoebus indeed spoken this concerning me ?

sections.label: Section 415
sections.original: ὥς φασιν οἱ μολόντες εἰς Θήβης πέδον .
sections.translation: So say the men who have come back to Thebes .

sections.label: Section 416
sections.original: παίδων τις οὖν ἤκουσε τῶν ἐμῶν τάδε ;
sections.translation: Has either of my sons heard this ?

sections.label: Section 417
sections.original: ἄμφω γʼ ὁμοίως , κἀξεπίστασθον καλῶς .
sections.translation: Yes , both have heard it , and know it well .

sections.label: Section 418
sections.original: κᾆθʼ οἱ κάκιστοι τῶνδʼ ἀκούσαντες , πάρος
sections.translation: And then those most evil of sons , aware of this , preferred the kingship to the wish of recalling me ?

sections.label: Section 420
sections.original: ἀλγῶ κλύουσα ταῦτʼ ἐγώ , φέρω δʼ ὅμως .
sections.translation: It grieves me to hear this , but I must bear it .

sections.label: Section 421
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οἱ θεοί σφιν μήτε τὴν πεπρωμένην
sections.translation: Then may the gods not quench their fated strife , and may it fall to me to decide this war on which they are now setting their hands , raising spear against spear !

sections.label: Section 425
sections.original: ὡς οὔτʼ ἂν ὃς νῦν σκῆπτρα καὶ θρόνους ἔχει
sections.translation: For then neither would he who now holds the scepter and the throne survive , nor would the exile ever return ; seeing that when I , their father , was being thrust without honor from my country , they did not stop or defend me . No , they saw me sent forth homeless ,

sections.label: Section 430
sections.original: αὐτοῖς ἐπέμφθην κἀξεκηρύχθην φυγάς .
sections.translation:
and heard the crier proclaim my sentence of exile .
Perhaps you will say that that was my own wish then , and that the city fittingly granted me that gift . Not so ! For on that first day , when my heart seethed ,

sections.label: Section 435
sections.original: τὸ κατθανεῖν ἦν καὶ τὸ λευσθῆναι πέτροις ,
sections.translation: and my sweetest wish was for death — indeed , death by stoning — no one was found to help me in that desire . But after a time , when all my anguish was now softened , and when I began to feel that my heart had been excessive in punishing those past errors ,

sections.label: Section 440
sections.original: τὸ τηνίκʼ ἤδη τοῦτο μὲν πόλις βίᾳ
sections.translation: then it was that the city set about to drive me by force from the land , after all that time . And my sons , when they had the strength to bring help — sons to their own father — they would not do it . For lack of one little word from them , I was left to wander , an outcast and a beggar forever .

sections.label: Section 445
sections.original: ἐκ ταῖνδε δʼ , οὔσαιν παρθένοιν , ὅσον φύσις
sections.translation: Instead , it is from these , maidens as they are , insofar as nature enables them , that I obtain my daily food , and a shelter in the land , and the aid of family . Their brothers have bartered their father for the throne , the scepter of power , and the rule of the realm .

sections.label: Section 450
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὔ τι μὴ λάχωσι τοῦδε συμμάχου ,
sections.translation: No , never will they win Oedipus for an ally , nor will good ever come to them from this reign at Thebes ; that I know , when I hear this maiden ’ s oracles and reflect on the old prophecies stored in my own mind , which Phoebus has fulfilled for me at last .

sections.label: Section 455
sections.original: πρὸς ταῦτα καὶ Κρέοντα πεμπόντων ἐμοῦ
sections.translation: Therefore let them send Creon to seek me — or whoever else is mighty in Thebes . For if you , strangers , with the help of the dread goddesses who reign among your district , are willing to defend me , you will obtain a great savior for this city ,

sections.label: Section 460
sections.original: σωτῆρʼ ἀρεῖσθε , τοῖς δʼ ἐμοῖς ἐχθροῖς πόνους .
sections.translation: and troubles for my enemies .

sections.label: Section 461
sections.original: ἐπάξιος μέν , Οἰδίπους , κατοικτίσαι ,
sections.translation: You are worthy of compassion , Oedipus , both you and these maidens . And since to this plea you append your power to save our land , I wish to advise you for your advantage .

sections.label: Section 465
sections.original: ὦ φίλταθʼ , ὡς νῦν πᾶν τελοῦντι προξένει .
sections.translation: Dearest friends , be my patrons , and I will bring everything to completion .

sections.label: Section 466
sections.original: θοῦ νῦν καθαρμὸν τῶνδε δαιμόνων , ἐφʼ ἃς
sections.translation: Then make atonement to these divinities , to whom you have come first , and on whose ground you have trespassed .

sections.label: Section 468
sections.original: τρόποισι ποίοις ; ὦ ξένοι , διδάσκετε .
sections.translation: With what rites ? Instruct me , strangers .
